Entertainment.headline: Spencer Pratt claims Leonardo DiCaprio and Jamie Foxx privately endorsed his mayoral bid
Entertainment.detail: Reality TV star Spencer Pratt claims that A-list actors Leonardo DiCaprio and Jamie Foxx are supporting his campaign to become the mayor of Los Angeles.

Entertainment.headline: Carmen Electra to pose for Playboy again at age 54
Entertainment.detail: Carmen Electra is set to pose for Playboy again, three decades after her first nude pictorial, marking a return to the magazine at the age of 54.
Matthew Perry's assistant has been sentenced in connection to the actor's death, and Keith Morrison provided exclusive reactions outside the courthouse.
Entertainment.headline: Matthew Perry Assistant Sentenced: Keith Morrison REACTS Outside Court (Exclusive)

Entertainment.detail: Movie star Keanu Reeves asks judge to show leniency.
Entertainment.headline: Movie star Keanu Reeves asks judge to show 'leniency'
John Reardon Returning to 'Hudson & Rex' After Cancer Battle, Surprise Exit
Entertainment.detail: John Reardon is set to return to 'Hudson & Rex' following a battle with cancer and a surprise exit from the show.

Zimbabwe.headline: China says trade talks on to address Zimbabwe's nontariff barrier concerns under zero-tariff scheme
Zimbabwe.detail: Trade talks are underway between China and Zimbabwe to address non-tariff barrier concerns under the zero-tariff scheme. This initiative aims to boost trade relations and remove obstacles for Zimbabwean businesses exporting to China.

Zimbabwe.headline: EMA bans lead in paint under new safety regulations
Zimbabwe.detail: The Environmental Management Agency (EMA) has banned the use of lead in paint manufacturing to safeguard public health and the environment. This new regulation aims to reduce lead exposure and its associated health risks.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Seals Oil Deal
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has entered into an oil deal, though specific details of the agreement are not provided in the search results. The deal is likely aimed at ensuring a stable supply of fuel and energy resources for the country.

Narendra Modi highlighted the importance of private sector involvement in Zimbabwe's industrial recovery. He emphasized that the private sector's innovation, investment, and efficiency are crucial for driving economic growth and development.
Zimbabwe.headline: Private sector key to industrial recovery: Modi
Govt races to meet wheat target as deadline looms
Zimbabwe.detail: The government is working to meet the wheat production target as the planting deadline approaches. Efforts are being made to ensure sufficient wheat supplies and reduce reliance on imports.

Science.headline: DNA Solves 250-year-old Mystery of the Seychelles' Lost Crocodiles
Science.detail: Scientists have solved the mystery of the Seychelles' vanished crocodiles using DNA from historic museum specimens, determining they were an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles that likely drifted thousands of kilometers from Africa or Madagascar.

Science.headline: Scottish Wrens May Be Evolving Into New Species Through Island Gigantism
Science.detail: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation, with some island birds growing astonishingly large and potentially evolving into a new species.

Science.headline: Ultrafast holographic imaging reveals electron and magnetic dynamics inside next-generation materials.
Science.detail: New ultrafast holographic imaging techniques allow scientists to visualize the dynamics of electrons and magnetism within advanced materials.

Science.headline: Heron-like, fish-eating dinosaur from 70 million years ago discovered in Argentina.
Science.detail: A new species of dinosaur with heron-like features, adapted for fish-eating, has been discovered in Argentina, dating back 70 million years.

Paleontologists have discovered a Cretaceous bird fossil in China that possessed a pair of tail feathers twice as long as its body, showcasing an extreme example of ornamentation in early avian evolution.
Science.headline: Cretaceous Bird from China Had Pair of Tail Feathers Twice as Long as Its Body

Finance & Economy.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit record closing highs
Finance & Economy.detail: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq reached record closing highs on Thursday following reports of a potential US-Iran ceasefire extension and the digestion of key inflation data, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average also finished at a closing high.

Finance & Economy.headline: Japan plans 'bridging bonds' to fund investment initiatives
Finance & Economy.detail: Japan is planning to utilize bridging bonds to finance Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's investment initiatives, according to the government's top spokesperson, a move that has raised concerns among investors already wary of increased debt issuance. These bonds are intended to cover temporary funding gaps, with the expectation of securing future funding sources.

Economist Luiz Pereira da Silva notes that China's tech-led growth and policy coordination are strengthening global stability, highlighting that China's governance model is well-equipped to manage global volatility by aligning investment, industrial policy, and long-term transformation. He views China's growth momentum as part of a structural transformation driven by investment and innovation, with policies closely aligned with the expansion of new sectors.
Finance & Economy.headline: Global Times: China's economic resilience backed up by policy coherence, new growth engines, says Luiz Awazu Pereira da Silva

Finance & Economy.detail: The United States has abandoned its 30-year policy towards China, with the Trump administration ceasing demands for political reform. U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer stated that the U.S. now accepts that China's political system is unlikely to be massively reformed, marking a significant shift from the Biden administration's approach.
Finance & Economy.headline: US Scraps 30-Year China Policy, Drops Demands for Political Reform

Finance & Economy.detail: The Philippines and Japan have committed to developing the Luzon Economic Corridor (LEC) into a world-class economic hub, recognizing its importance in strengthening regional economic architecture and global supply chain resilience. President Marcos Jr. and Prime Minister Takaichi pledged to enhance cooperation on infrastructure and economic initiatives within the LEC framework, including rail and port modernization, clean energy, and digital connectivity.
Finance & Economy.headline: PH, Japan vow to develop 'world-class' Luzon Economic Corridor

Politics.headline: President Cyril Ramaphosa officiates title deed handover ceremony in North West
Politics.detail: President Ramaphosa will oversee the upgrading of land tenure rights and the handover of title deeds to several communities in the North West Province, marking a step in addressing historical land dispossession.
Canada's Minister of Foreign Affairs, Anita Anand, will meet with China's Minister of Foreign Affairs, Wang Yi, in Ottawa for a bilateral meeting.
Politics.headline: Minister Anand to meet with China's Minister of Foreign Affairs

Politics.detail: The Japanese government has formally announced that the supplementary budget bill is scheduled to be submitted on June 3rd.
Politics.headline: Japan's Government officially announces that supplementary budget bill will be submitted on June 3rd

Politics.detail: Iran and the U.S. have reportedly reached a tentative agreement to extend their truce for 60 days, but it is pending approval from President Trump.
Politics.headline: Iran and U.S. reach deal to extend ceasefire, pending Trump's approval

Politics.detail: In Minnesota, Democrats and Republicans are holding their state party conventions to decide who will represent their parties in the upcoming elections for governor, U.S. Senate, and other offices.
Politics.headline: Democrats and Republicans convene for their party conventions

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A timely call to end Red-tagging
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Commission on Human Rights (CHR) called for an end to Red-tagging, which is the pattern of labeling individuals and groups as 'communists,' 'terrorists,' or 'enemies of the state,' often without due process.
This editorial discusses the importance of focusing on jobs and training to improve the prospects for young people.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on jobs and training: boosting young people's chances should be a national mission
The Guardian view on energy shocks: winter is coming – and Labour needs a plan
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial emphasizes the need for the Labour party to develop a plan to address potential energy shocks in the coming winter.
The editorial argues that following Tony Blair's past policy approaches is not a viable strategy for Labour's future success.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Tony Blair's advice for Labour: policymaking like it's 1999 will not lead to a revival
4Ps, electricity bills, and Issues in social program funding
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article discusses issues related to the funding and implementation of social programs, including the 4Ps and concerns about electricity bills.

Education.detail: Saint Louis University (SLU) partnered with the Local Government Unit of San Quintin to conduct a training workshop on Instructional Material Preparation for Child Development Workers, focusing on designing multisensory instructional materials aligned with the Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD) curriculum. The training aimed to create engaging and inclusive learning environments for the municipality's youngest learners.
Education.headline: Empowering Early Educators: SLU and San Quintin partner to elevate Child Development through Multisensory Learning Materials
Russia-China Year of Education creates new opportunities for youth: Russian spokesperson
Education.detail: The Russia-China Year of Education provides a platform for talent cultivation and participation in academic activities for young professionals. The project aims to enhance mutual understanding between the two countries and deepen knowledge of the partner country among young people.

Education.headline: Michigan celebrates 529 Day, Recognizing Importance of Saving for Education and Future Opportunities
Education.detail: Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer proclaimed May 29 as “529 Day” to encourage families to save for education and career training. The Michigan Education Trust (MET) is offering a bonus to help families start saving, emphasizing that savings can open doors for future opportunities.

UNESCO reports that AI is rapidly reshaping higher education in South Asia, creating opportunities but also raising questions about policy, faculty capacities, and ethical governance. UNESCO is supporting commitment towards a gender-equitable AI transformation in higher education.
Education.headline: Advancing ethical and innovative AI integration in higher education in South Asia

Education.detail: Florida State University's Learning Systems Institute (LSI) will collaborate with IREX on the Early Grade Education Activity in Jordan, supporting initiatives to address low literacy and numeracy rates. LSI will support Jordanian universities through curriculum review, faculty development, and research.
Education.headline: Learning Systems Institute at FSU chosen to collaborate on U.S. Department of State-funded Early Grade Education Activity in Jordan

Sports.detail: Darts star Luke Littler revealed that crowd abuse almost made him quit during the Premier League Darts final, leading to an emotional breakdown. He has been facing relentless heckling and booing from sections of the audience, which has taken a toll on his performance and mental state.
Sports.headline: Luke Littler breaks down in tears after Premier League Darts final as star reveals crowd abuse had him ready to quit
World number one Jannik Sinner sensationally knocked out of Roland Garros
Sports.detail: Jannik Sinner, the world's number one tennis player, experienced a shocking defeat at Roland Garros. His unexpected exit marks a major upset in the tournament.

Sports.headline: Clarke signs on until 2030 World Cup - a gamble SFA had to take?
Sports.detail: Steve Clarke has signed a new contract that extends through the 2030 World Cup. This decision is viewed as a significant commitment by the Scottish Football Association.

Sports.headline: Konaté poised to leave after failing to agree new contract
Sports.detail: Ibrahima Konaté is likely to leave Liverpool after failing to reach an agreement on a new contract. His potential departure would be a significant change for the team's defensive lineup.

Sports.headline: Praggnanandhaa Stuns World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen At Norway Chess - Video
Sports.detail: Young Indian Grandmaster Praggnanandhaa defeated world number one Magnus Carlsen at the Norway Chess tournament. This victory is a significant achievement for the rising star in the world of chess.

Lifestyle.detail: The singer addressed criticism of her fashion choices, highlighting concerns about the hypersexualization of young girls.
Lifestyle.headline: Olivia Rodrigo responds to babydoll dress criticism: 'It shows how we normalize pedophilia in our culture'
Whispered wisdom of a parent's last words
Lifestyle.detail: A reflective piece on the profound impact and lasting guidance found in the final words of a parent.

Lifestyle.headline: Actress Carrie Wong to star alongside Lee Joon-gi and Kentaro Sakaguchi in new Viu drama Kidnap Game
Lifestyle.detail: Mediacorp actress Carrie Wong will play a flight attendant in the upcoming drama Kidnap Game, which tells the story of seven kidnapping cases occurring simultaneously in seven Asian cities.
The latest watches from Chanel, IWC, and Parmigiani Fleurier showcase the forward pushing innovation in mechanical watchmaking.
Lifestyle.headline: The innovative new watches from Watches and Wonders 2026

Lifestyle.detail: Some parents are turning to cremation to grieve a loss that can otherwise feel invisible.
Lifestyle.headline: After early miscarriage, some parents turn to cremation for closure: ‘It made the pregnancy loss feel real’

Technology.headline: Google I/O 2026 Signals an Extinction Event for Standalone Apps
Technology.detail: Google I/O 2026 revealed a far more aggressive AI strategy than many expected, potentially signaling the end for standalone apps as we know them.

Technology.headline: Telecom Heavyweights Circle Wagons Around Cybersecurity
Technology.detail: Major telecom providers are launching a cybersecurity alliance to improve real-time threat sharing and strengthen defenses against escalating attacks on communications infrastructure.
New MRI sensors have been developed to detect target molecules in the brain and body with greater sensitivity, potentially improving diagnostics.
Technology.headline: New MRI sensors detect target molecules in the brain and body with high sensitivity

Technology.detail: A lab-grown model of the brain and spinal cord suggests that nerve damage previously considered irreversible might be reversed, offering new hope for treating spinal injuries.
Technology.headline: Lab-grown brain-spinal cord model shows 'irreversible' nerve damage may be reversed

Technology.detail: Pope Leo XIV warns of the dangers of artificial intelligence in his first encyclical, stating that as civilization grapples with the power of AI, the main challenge is remaining "profoundly human."
Technology.headline: Pope Leo calls for "disarming" of AI in technology-focused encyclical
